first, the switch 2 or steam deck or rog ally should not be compared directly with a home console like the ps5 or xbox. none of them are designed to deliver the level of performance comparable to home console. the switch 2’s success is driven less by raw power and more by its combination of portability, convenience, and strong first-party exclusives. it is not simply a case of the hardware being underpowered. in fact, the switch 2 is capable enough to handle demanding titles like resident evil 9 and pragmata better than the steam deck. look up youtube videos. the bigger limitation is often whether studios choose to release their games on nintendo hardware at all, or whether older titles receive proper updates and optimization for the new system. that is similar to how there are still many playstation-exclusive titles that you cannot access on the steam deck. that said more third-party titles are now making their way to the switch 2.
